diagnosis.differential_diagnosis: Mucosal lichen planus, leukoedema, linea alba, bitten mucosa, follicular keratosis, dyskeratosis congenita (DKC), congenital pachyonychia, focal epithelial hyperplasia, systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE), vegetative pioestomatitis, proliferative verrucous leukoplakia (PVL), oral florid papillomatosis, mucosal syphilids (mucous plaques), candidiasis, leukoplakia, frictional keratosis, squamous cell carcinoma
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was supported by family history, cytological findings showing perinuclear eosinophilic condensation of epithelial cells, and histological findings including hyperparakeratosis and acanthosis with vacuolization of keratinocytes in the suprabasal layers. The patients' age and the bilateral presentation of lesions in the oral mucosa were also key factors in ruling out other conditions such as mucosal lichen planus, which typically appears later in life.
